import MethodEndpoint from "@theme/ApiExplorer/MethodEndpoint";
import ParamsDetails from "@theme/ParamsDetails";

import StatusCodes from "@theme/StatusCodes";
import OperationTabs from "@theme/OperationTabs";

<Heading
  as={"h1"}
  className={"openapi__heading"}
  children={"Send a message"}
>
</Heading>

<MethodEndpoint
  method={"post"}
  path={"/v1/messages"}
  context={"endpoint"}
>
  
</MethodEndpoint>



Send a communication to a recipient. The `channel` field controls delivery:

- Specify a channel (e.g. `email`, `sms`) to send directly to that channel.
- Use `channel: auto` to trigger smart routing — the platform resolves the best
  channel based on the recipient's preferences.

Recipients are identified either by `partyId` (for known customers) or by
providing `contactDetails` directly (for unknown parties).

A `202 Accepted` response means the message has been accepted and queued —
not necessarily delivered. Poll `GET /v1/messages/{messageId}` or subscribe
to Kafka events for delivery confirmation.

  id={"request"}
  as={"h2"}
  className={"openapi-tabs__heading"}
>
  <Translate id="theme.openapi.request.title">Request</Translate>
</Heading>
